analysiskmeans 0.0.0.9000
    • Getting Started with analysiskmeans!
    • Package Installation Guide
    • Package Functions
    • Command Line Interface
    • Acknowledgements

Conduct K Means Clustering

Source: R/kmeans.R
k_means.Rd

Conduct K Means Clustering

Usage

k_means(min_k, max_k, n_starts = 25, seed = 42, pca)

Arguments

min_k

The min amount k value you wish to start at

max_k

The max amount k value you wish to go up to

n_starts

Number of initial sets

seed

Random seed for reproducibility

pca

Resulting PCA matrix

Value

List Average silhouette scores and of K Means clustering results with various K values

Examples

library(analysiskmeans)
data(example_sce)
SingleCellExperiment::logcounts(example_sce) <- log1p(SingleCellExperiment::counts(example_sce))#Line taken from Claude AI - Debug
sce <- example_sce
results <- data_config(sce)
#> Warning: 'librarySizeFactors' is deprecated.
#> Use 'scrapper::centerSizeFactors' instead.
#> See help("Deprecated")
#> Warning: 'normalizeCounts' is deprecated.
#> Use 'scrapper::normalizeCounts' instead.
#> See help("Deprecated")
mat_norm <- top_x_genes(results$sce, n_top = 50)
pca <- computepca(mat_norm)
k_means(4, 9, n_starts = 25, seed = 42, pca)
#> $metrics
#>   k      wss avg_silhouette
#> 1 4 689.9560     0.05556822
#> 2 5 625.5026     0.06131297
#> 3 6 565.5828     0.06596026
#> 4 7 512.3719     0.06487671
#> 5 8 459.8210     0.06096352
#> 6 9 408.9954     0.06596343
#> 
#> $km_list
#> $km_list$`4`
#> K-means clustering with 4 clusters of sizes 5, 6, 4, 5
#> 
#> Cluster means:
#>          PC1        PC2         PC3        PC4        PC5        PC6
#> 1  0.8232067 -0.8839124  0.04523608  2.6112471 -0.9545080 -0.1528163
#> 2  1.6886721  2.5539593 -0.06304373 -1.0985761 -0.5922869  0.1821593
#> 3  0.6230835 -3.3298738 -0.59296210 -1.1663800  0.4735455  0.6938414
#> 4 -3.3480799  0.4830603  0.50478608 -0.3598518  1.2864159 -0.6208480
#>             PC7         PC8         PC9       PC10        PC11        PC12
#> 1 -0.1888941132  0.76621593  0.04956604  0.2115829 -0.24864958 -0.33774825
#> 2  0.0004984321  0.18664874 -0.15596771 -0.2554676  0.02378879  0.09522327
#> 3 -0.0821085803 -1.16140895 -0.30747673 -0.7436367  0.30815670  0.37444744
#> 4  0.2539828590 -0.06106726  0.38357659  0.6898875 -0.02642233 -0.07607763
#>         PC13        PC14       PC15         PC16         PC17        PC18
#> 1 -0.2801969 -0.30766032 -0.1919007  0.471046708 -0.105349781  0.04905483
#> 2  0.2591522  0.26934504  0.1066981 -0.197540193  0.032224804 -0.16374325
#> 3 -0.3959851  0.10326587  0.2675799 -0.282831961  0.094656634 -0.07383421
#> 4  0.2860023 -0.09816642 -0.1502010 -0.007732908 -0.009045291  0.20650444
#>          PC19         PC20
#> 1  0.03405607 4.773770e-16
#> 2  0.04369059 3.139096e-16
#> 3 -0.15219664 2.383870e-16
#> 4  0.03527252 5.136058e-16
#> 
#> Clustering vector:
#>  [1] 2 2 1 1 4 4 2 2 3 4 3 4 2 4 1 1 3 3 1 2
#> 
#> Within cluster sum of squares by cluster:
#> [1] 166.1667 219.2782 113.2901 191.2211
#>  (between_SS / total_SS =  27.4 %)
#> 
#> Available components:
#> 
#> [1] "cluster"      "centers"      "totss"        "withinss"     "tot.withinss"
#> [6] "betweenss"    "size"         "iter"         "ifault"      
#> 
#> $km_list$`5`
#> K-means clustering with 5 clusters of sizes 6, 1, 4, 5, 4
#> 
#> Cluster means:
#>          PC1         PC2         PC3        PC4         PC5        PC6
#> 1  1.6886721  2.55395926 -0.06304373 -1.0985761 -0.59228688  0.1821593

#> [1] 219.2782   0.0000 126.7676 166.1667 113.2901
#>  (between_SS / total_SS =  34.2 %)

#> 
#> [1] "cluster"      "centers"      "totss"        "withinss"     "tot.withinss"
#> [6] "betweenss"    "size"         "iter"         "ifault"
